'''Adrian Purtell''' (born 31 January 1985) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for the [[Albury Thunder]].

He previously played for the [[Canberra Raiders]], [[Penrith Panthers]], [[Bradford Bulls]] and the [[London Broncos]]. His usual position is as a {{rlp|ce}}.

==Career==
===Early career===
Purtell played junior rugby league with [[Albury Thunder|Albury Rams]] and [[Lavington Panthers]].<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Border Mail|title=Purtell's pleasure at Panther coup|author=Brett Kohlhagen|url=http://www.bordermail.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/purtells-pleasure-at-panther-coup/1874724.aspx|access-date=12 July 2010|date=2 July 2010}}</ref> 
[[File:AdrianPurtell.jpg|thumb|right|Purtell playing for the Canberra Raiders]]

===Canberra Raiders===
He made his début for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in round 1 2006 against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].  In 2006, he was crowned the club's Rookie of the Year. During the season, he also re-signed with the club until the end of 2008.

In round 6 of the [[2007 NRL season]], Purtell was shifted from the wing to the centres. Purtell took this opportunity with both hands, scoring two tries in his first match in the new position and showing consistent form after that match.

Purtell was diagnosed with [[deep vein thrombosis]] in the middle of 2007.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=ABC News|location=Australia|title=Raider Purtell sidelined with DVT|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2007/06/02/1940646.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080416025706/http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2007/06/02/1940646.htm|url-status=dead|archive-date=16 April 2008|access-date=12 July 2010|date=2 June 2007}}</ref> He missed the rest of the season as a result. This came at a cruel time for Purtell as he had only just cemented a spot in the centres. In his last game of 2007, he scored 3 tries against the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], including a 90-metre solo effort.{{cn|date=June 2025}}

After a long recovery Purtell made a successful return to rugby league. On 16 March 2008, Purtell completed his return by crossing over for 2 tries against the [[Newcastle Knights]]. Soon after he re-signed to stay with Canberra until the end of the 2011 season, rejecting an offer to join the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|title=Raiders re-sign Purtell|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/raiders-re-sign-purtell/story-e6frep96-1111116271641|access-date=12 July 2010|date=7 May 2008}}</ref> He ended the [[2008 NRL season]] as top try-scorer for the Raiders.

After being unable to maintain his position in the starting team for the second half of the 2009 season, Purtell was offered a release by Canberra.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Border Mail|title=Adrian Purtell set to quit Canberra Raiders|author=Matt Cram|url=http://www.bordermail.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/adrian-purtell-set-to-quit-canberra-raiders/1610983.aspx|access-date=12 July 2010|date=1 September 2009}}</ref>

===Penrith Panthers===
He joined the [[Penrith Panthers]] from the [[2010 NRL season]].  In his first year at the club, Penrith finished second on the table but were eliminated from the finals series after losing both matches consecutively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2010/09/28/2010-season-review-penrith-panthers/|title=2010 SEASON REVIEW: Penrith Panthers|website=nrl.com|access-date=6 January 2020|archive-date=28 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201028120738/https://www.nrl.com/news/2010/09/28/2010-season-review-penrith-panthers/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

The following year, Penrith finished a disappointing 12th on the table and missed out on the finals.
[[File:Adrian Purtell.jpg|thumb|right|Purtell training with the Bradford Bulls]]

===Bradford Bulls===
It was announced{{when|date=June 2025}} that Purtell signed a 3-year contract with [[Super League]] team [[Bradford Bulls]].{{cn|date=June 2025}}

'''2012''' – [[2012 Bradford Bulls season|2012 Season]]

Purtell featured in two of the four pre-season friendlies. He played against [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].

Adrian featured in two consecutive games from Round 1 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to Round 2 ([[Castleford Tigers]]). Purtell was injured for Rounds 3–6. He returned in Round 7 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]). He was ill for Round 8. He once again returned and featured in four consecutive games from Round 9 ([[Hull F.C.]]) to Round 12 ([[Huddersfield Giants]]). He was injured for Rounds 13–14. He returned to feature in Round 15 ([[Leeds Rhinos]]). Purtell also appeared in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] and [[Warrington Wolves]].

'''2013''' – [[2013 Bradford Bulls season|2013 Season]]

Purtell missed the pre-season games against [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Leeds Rhinos]] due to the doctors not giving him permission to play until April.{{cn|date=June 2025}}

Due to the doctors orders he missed Rounds 1–6.{{cn|date=June 2025}} He made his comeback and played in ten consecutive games from Round 7 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) to Round 16 ([[Huddersfield Giants]]) however he missed Rounds 17-18 due to injury but returned to play in Round 19 ([[Widnes Vikings]]) to Round 25 ([[Castleford Tigers]]). Purtell also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[London Broncos]]. 

'''2014''' – [[2014 Bradford Bulls season|2014 Season]]

Purtell featured in the pre-season games against Hull F.C. and Castleford Tigers.

He featured in Round 1 ([[Castleford Tigers]]) to Round 21 (Huddersfield Giants). Adrian also featured in Round 4 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.]]) to the Quarter Final (Warrington Wolves) in the [[Challenge Cup]]. Purtell scored against [[Castleford Tigers]] (1 try), [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] (3 tries), [[Hull F.C.]] (1 try), [[Salford Red Devils]] (1 try), [[Catalans Dragons]] (1 try), Warrington Wolves (3 tries) and [[St Helens R.F.C.]] (1 try).{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

He signed a new 1 Year deal with Bradford despite their relegation from [[Super League]].{{cn|date=June 2025}}

'''2015''' – [[2015 Bradford Bulls season|2015 Season]]

Purtell featured in the pre-season friendlies against Castleford Tigers and [[Leeds Rhinos]].

He featured in Round 1 ([[Leigh Centurions]]) to Round 4 ([[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]]) then in Round 6 ([[Workington Town]]) to Round 9 ([[London Broncos]]). Purtell also played in Round 11 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]) to Round 13 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]) then in Round 22 ([[Leigh Centurions]]). Purtell played in Qualifier 1 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]) to Qualifier 6 ([[Leigh Centurions]]). Adrian played in the £1 Million Game ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]). He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 4 ([[Workington Town]]). He scored against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] (3 tries), [[Featherstone Rovers]] (1 try), [[Workington Town]] (3 tries), [[Sheffield Eagles]] (1 try), [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (2 tries), [[Dewsbury Rams]] (2 tries), [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] (2 tries), [[Salford Red Devils]] (1 try) and [[Widnes Vikings]] (1 try).{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

'''2016''' – [[2016 Bradford Bulls season|2016 Season]]

After the retirement of [[Chev Walker]] it was announced that Purtell would be the captain of the Bulls. He did not feature in any of the friendlies due to a minor injury.{{cn|date=June 2025}}

Purtell featured in Round 1 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]) to Round 4 ([[Leigh Centurions]]) then in Round 9 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]) to Round 11 ([[Workington Town]]). He played in the Championship Shield Game 2 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]) then in the Final ([[Sheffield Eagles]]).

===London Broncos===
'''2017''' – [[2017 RFL Championship|2017 Season]]

Purtell signed to play for London Broncos in the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12212/10552455/london-broncos-sign-jay-pitts-from-bradford-bulls|title=London Broncos sign Jay Pitts from Bradford Bulls|publisher=Sky Sports|date=26 August 2016|access-date=20 February 2017}}</ref>

Purtell featured in Round 1 ([[Swinton Lions]]) to Round 7 ([[Rochdale Hornets]]). He also featured in the Challenge Cup in Round 4 ([[Toronto Wolfpack]]).

==Albury Thunder==
Adrian Purtell returned to his hometown to play [[Albury Thunder]] in [[Group 9 Rugby League|Group 9]] of the [[New South Wales Rugby League]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

<!--===Heart attack===
{{unreferencedsect|date=June 2025}}
On 27 May 2012, Purtell suffered a heart attack. Club medical staff were aware of Purtell's chest pains, and stopped many times for treatment, as the Bulls returned from their 37–22 loss to Leeds Rhinos. He was cleared to resume playing in April 2013.-->
